cases.passage: Rahul had been planning his trip to Europe for several months. He had saved enough money to buy a one-way ticket to London. On the day of his departure, he woke up early, got dressed, and had a quick breakfast. He then packed his bags and headed to the airport. As he was checking in for his flight, he realized that he had forgotten to pack his passport. He quickly returned home to fetch it, and then rushed back to the airport to catch his flight.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'Rahul had been planning his trip to Europe for several months'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'Rahul had been planning his trip to Europe for several months' is in the Present Perfect Continuous tense, as it describes an action that started in the past and continues up to the present moment.

questions.q: What is the voice of the sentence 'Rahul had been planning his trip to Europe for several months'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'Rahul had been planning his trip to Europe for several months' is in the Active Voice, as the subject 'Rahul' is performing the action of planning.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'He had saved enough money to buy a one-way ticket to London'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'He had saved enough money to buy a one-way ticket to London' is in the Present Perfect Simple tense, as it describes an action that happened at an unspecified time in the past.

questions.q: What is the voice of the sentence 'He then packed his bags and headed to the airport'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'He then packed his bags and headed to the airport' is in the Active Voice, as the subject 'He' is performing the action of packing and heading.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'As he was checking in for his flight, he realized that he had forgotten to pack his passport'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'As he was checking in for his flight, he realized that he had forgotten to pack his passport' is in the Simple Present tense, as it describes an action that is happening at the moment of speaking.

cases.passage: The company had been facing financial difficulties for several years. The board of directors decided to downsize the company by laying off 20% of its employees. The employees were shocked and saddened by the news. They had been working for the company for many years and had grown attached to their colleagues. The company's management assured them that they would receive generous severance packages and outplacement assistance. However, the employees were still worried about their future.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'The company had been facing financial difficulties for several years'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'The company had been facing financial difficulties for several years' is in the Present Perfect Continuous tense, as it describes an action that started in the past and continues up to the present moment.

questions.q: What is the voice of the sentence 'The employees were shocked and saddened by the news'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'The employees were shocked and saddened by the news' is in the Active Voice, as the subject 'employees' is performing the action of being shocked and saddened.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'They had been working for the company for many years'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'They had been working for the company for many years' is in the Present Perfect Continuous tense, as it describes an action that started in the past and continues up to the present moment.

questions.q: What is the voice of the sentence 'The company's management assured them that they would receive generous severance packages and outplacement assistance'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'The company's management assured them that they would receive generous severance packages and outplacement assistance' is in the Active Voice, as the subject 'management' is performing the action of assuring.

questions.q: What tense has been used in the sentence 'However, the employees were still worried about their future'?
questions.explanation: The sentence 'However, the employees were still worried about their future' is in the Simple Present tense, as it describes an action that is happening at the moment of speaking.
